2605.21311.title: DeCoR: Design and Control Co-Optimization for Urban Streets Using Reinforcement Learning
2605.21311.paper_summary: Modern vision systems can detect, track, and forecast urban actors at scale, yet translating perception outputs to urban design remains limited. We introduce DeCoR, a two-stage reinforcement learning framework that leverages flow observations to co-optimize crosswalk layout and network-level signal control. The design stage encodes the pedestrian network as a graph and learns a generative policy that parameterizes a Gaussian mixture model over crosswalk location and width, from which new crosswalks are sampled. For each layout, a shared control policy learns adaptive signal timings to minimize joint pedestrian and vehicle delay. On a 750 m real-world urban corridor with demand sensed from video and Wi-Fi logs, DeCoR learns a layout that reduces pedestrian arrival time to their nearest crosswalk by 23% while using fewer crosswalks than existing configurations. On the control side, DeCoR reduces pedestrian and vehicle wait time by 79% and 65%, respectively, relative to fixed-time signalization. Further, the control policy generalizes to demands outside of training and is robust to layout changes without retraining.
2605.21311.paper_summary_zh: 現代視覺系統可以在大規模上檢測、追蹤和預測城市行為者，但將感知輸出轉化為城市設計仍然有限。我們介紹DeCoR，一種兩階段的強化學習框架，利用流量觀測來共同優化人行道佈局和網絡級信號控制。設計階段將行人網絡編碼為圖形，並學習一種生成策略，該策略參數化一個高斯混合模型，涵蓋人行道的位置和寬度，從中抽樣出新的行人道。對於每個佈局，共享控制策略學習自適應信號時序，以最小化行人和車輛的聯合延遲。在一個750米的現實世界城市走廊中，根據視頻和Wi-Fi日誌感知需求，DeCoR學習到一種佈局，將行人到達最近人行道的時間縮短了23%，同時使用的行人道數量少於現有配置。在控制方面，DeCoR相對於固定時間信號化，分別減少了79%和65%的行人和車輛等待時間。此外，控制策略對訓練之外的需求具有良好的泛化能力，並且對佈局變更具有穩健性，無需重新訓練。
2605.21311.author: Bibek Poudel et.al.
2605.21311.authors: Bibek Poudel, Lei Zhu, Kevin Heaslip, Sai Swaminathan, Weizi Li
